1	import { render, screen, fireEvent, within } from '@testing-library/react'
2	import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest'
3	import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js'
4	import Board from './Board.jsx'
5	
6	vi.mock('../../hooks/useBoard.js', () => ({ useBoard: vi.fn() }))
7	
8	const MOCK_CARD = {
9	  id: 'c1',
10	  title: 'Test Card',
11	  assignee: 'Alice',
12	  description: 'A description',
13	  column: 'ready',
14	  position: 1,
15	  created_at: Date.now(),
16	  comments: [],
17	}
18	
19	const DEFAULT_STATE = {
20	  cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] },
21	  loading: false,
22	  error: null,
23	  createCard: vi.fn(),
24	  updateCard: vi.fn(),
25	  deleteCard: vi.fn(),
26	  moveCard: vi.fn(),
27	  addComment: vi.fn(),
28	}
29	
30	beforeEach(() => {
31	  useBoard.mockReturnValue(DEFAULT_STATE)
32	})
33	
34	describe('Board', () => {
35	  it('renders three column regions', () => {
36	    render(<Board />)
37	    expect(screen.getAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(3)
38	  })
39	
40	  it('renders Ready column heading', () => {
41	    render(<Board />)
42	    expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Ready' })).toBeInTheDocument()
43	  })
44	
45	  it('renders In Progress column heading', () => {
46	    render(<Board />)
47	    expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'In Progress' })).toBeInTheDocument()
48	  })
49	
50	  it('renders Done column heading', () => {
51	    render(<Board />)
52	    expect(screen.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Done' })).toBeInTheDocument()
53	  })
54	
55	  it('shows loading indicator when loading is true', () => {
56	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, loading: true })
57	    render(<Board />)
58	    expect(screen.getByLabelText('Loading')).toBeInTheDocument()
59	    expect(screen.queryAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(0)
60	  })
61	
62	  it('shows error banner when error is set', () => {
63	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, error: 'fetch failed' })
64	    render(<Board />)
65	    expect(screen.getByRole('alert')).toHaveTextContent('fetch failed')
66	  })
67	
68	  it('passes ready cards to Ready column', () => {
69	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({
70	      ...DEFAULT_STATE,
71	      cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] },
72	    })
73	    render(<Board />)
74	    expect(
75	      within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Ready' })).getByText('Test Card')
76	    ).toBeInTheDocument()
77	  })
78	
79	  it('passes in_progress cards to In Progress column', () => {
80	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({
81	      ...DEFAULT_STATE,
82	      cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c2', column: 'in-progress' }], done: [] },
83	    })
84	    render(<Board />)
85	    expect(
86	      within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'In Progress' })).getByText('Test Card')
87	    ).toBeInTheDocument()
88	  })
89	
90	  it('passes done cards to Done column', () => {
91	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({
92	      ...DEFAULT_STATE,
93	      cards: { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [{ ...MOCK_CARD, id: 'c3', column: 'done' }] },
94	    })
95	    render(<Board />)
96	    expect(
97	      within(screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Done' })).getByText('Test Card')
98	    ).toBeInTheDocument()
99	  })
100	
101	  it('opens modal when a card is clicked', () => {
102	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({
103	      ...DEFAULT_STATE,
104	      cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] },
105	    })
106	    render(<Board />)
107	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))
108	    expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toBeInTheDocument()
109	  })
110	
111	  it('closes modal when close button is clicked', () => {
112	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({
113	      ...DEFAULT_STATE,
114	      cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] },
115	    })
116	    render(<Board />)
117	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Test Card' }))
118	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /close/i }))
119	    expect(screen.queryByRole('dialog')).toBeNull()
120	  })
121	
122	  it('modal reflects updated card data when useBoard cards state changes', () => {
123	    const card = { ...MOCK_CARD, title: 'Old Title' }
124	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({
125	      ...DEFAULT_STATE,
126	      cards: { ready: [card], in_progress: [], done: [] },
127	    })
128	    const { rerender } = render(<Board />)
129	    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: 'Old Title' }))
130	    expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toHaveTextContent('Old Title')
131	
132	    // Simulate useBoard updating the card in place (e.g. after updateCard resolves)
133	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({
134	      ...DEFAULT_STATE,
135	      cards: { ready: [{ ...card, title: 'New Title' }], in_progress: [], done: [] },
136	    })
137	    rerender(<Board />)
138	    expect(screen.getByRole('dialog')).toHaveTextContent('New Title')
139	  })
140	
141	  it('modal closes automatically when selected card is removed from cards state', () => {
142	    useBoard.mockReturnValue({
143	      ...DEFAULT_STATE,
144	      cards: { ready: [MOCK_CARD], in_progress: [], done: [] },
145	    })

The second approach feels cleaner since it avoids changing Column's interface.

1	import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
2	import {
3	  fetchCards,
4	  createCard as apiCreateCard,
5	  updateCard as apiUpdateCard,
6	  deleteCard as apiDeleteCard,
7	  createComment,
8	} from '../api/client.js'
9	
10	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
11	// Module-level helpers
12	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	
14	/**
15	 * Map API column value → state key.
16	 * 'in-progress' (hyphen) → 'in_progress' (underscore); others pass through.
17	 * @param {string} col
18	 * @returns {string}
19	 */
20	export function columnToKey(col) {
21	  return col === 'in-progress' ? 'in_progress' : col
22	}
23	
24	/**
25	 * Group a flat array of cards into the board state shape, sorted by position.
26	 * @param {import('../api/client.js').Card[]} cards
27	 * @returns {{ ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }}
28	 */
29	function groupCards(cards) {
30	  const board = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
31	  for (const card of cards) {
32	    const key = columnToKey(card.column)
33	    if (key in board) board[key].push(card)
34	  }
35	  for (const key of Object.keys(board)) {
36	    board[key].sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
37	  }
38	  return board
39	}
40	
41	const EMPTY_BOARD = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
42	
43	// Temp-ID counter for optimistic records (module-level so it's stable across renders)
44	let _tempId = 0
45	function nextTempId() {
46	  return '__temp_' + (++_tempId)
47	}
48	
49	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	// useBoard hook
51	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	
53	/**
54	 * Manages board state: cards grouped by column, loading, and error.
55	 *
56	 * Column convention
57	 *   - State keys:  ready | in_progress | done  (underscore)
58	 *   - API values:  ready | in-progress  | done  (hyphen for in-progress)
59	 *   - All hook functions that accept a column name expect the API format.
60	 *   - Each card's .column field retains the API format.
61	 *
62	 * @returns {{
63	 *   cards: { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] },
64	 *   loading: boolean,
65	 *   error: string|null,
66	 *   createCard: (data: object) => Promise<Card>,
67	 *   updateCard: (id: string, data: object) => Promise<Card>,
68	 *   deleteCard: (id: string) => Promise<void>,
69	 *   moveCard: (id: string, targetColumn: string, position?: number) => Promise<Card>,
70	 *   addComment: (cardId: string, data: object) => Promise<Comment>,
71	 * }}
72	 */
73	export function useBoard() {
74	  const [cards, setCards] = useState(EMPTY_BOARD)
75	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
76	  const [error, setError] = useState(null)
77	
78	  // Counter for concurrent in-flight operations so loading stays true
79	  // until the LAST operation finishes.
80	  const pendingRef = useRef(0)
81	
82	  // Ref that always holds the latest cards state so we can read it
83	  // synchronously (functional updaters are called lazily by React, which
84	  // would leave a rollback variable null if the API rejects as a microtask
85	  // before the updater runs).
86	  const cardsRef = useRef(EMPTY_BOARD)
87	
88	  /**
89	   * Apply a state update eagerly: compute the next value synchronously from
90	   * cardsRef.current, update the ref, then call setCards with the plain
91	   * computed value (not a function) so React picks it up on the next render.
92	   */
93	  function applyCards(updater) {
94	    const next = typeof updater === 'function' ? updater(cardsRef.current) : updater
95	    cardsRef.current = next
96	    setCards(next)
97	  }
98	
99	  function beginOp() { pendingRef.current++; setLoading(true) }
100	  function endOp()   { if (--pendingRef.current === 0) setLoading(false) }
101	
102	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	  // Initial data load
104	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	
106	  useEffect(() => {
107	    let cancelled = false
108	    setError(null)
109	    beginOp()
110	
111	    fetchCards()
112	      .then(data => {
113	        if (cancelled) return
114	        applyCards(groupCards(data))
115	        endOp()
116	      })
117	      .catch(err => {
118	        if (cancelled) return
119	        setError(err.message)
120	        endOp()
121	      })
122	
123	    return () => { cancelled = true }
124	  }, [])
125	
126	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	  // createCard — temp-ID optimistic pattern
128	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	
130	  const createCard = useCallback(async (data) => {
131	    const tempId = nextTempId()
132	    const col = data.column ?? 'ready'
133	    const key = columnToKey(col)
134	
135	    const optimisticCard = {
136	      id: tempId,
137	      title: data.title,
138	      assignee: data.assignee ?? null,
139	      column: col,
140	      position: Infinity,
141	      description: data.description ?? null,
142	      created_at: Date.now(),
143	      comments: [],
144	    }
145	
146	    applyCards(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: [...prev[key], optimisticCard] }))
147	    beginOp()
148	
149	    try {
150	      const created = await apiCreateCard(data)
151	      const createdKey = columnToKey(created.column)
152	      applyCards(prev => {
153	        const next = { ...prev }
154	        // Remove the temp card from whichever column it was placed in
155	        next[key] = next[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId)
156	        // Insert the server card into the column the server assigned
157	        next[createdKey] = [...next[createdKey], created]
158	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
159	        return next
160	      })
161	      return created
162	    } catch (err) {
163	      applyCards(prev => ({
164	        ...prev,
165	        [key]: prev[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId),
166	      }))
167	      throw err
168	    } finally {
169	      endOp()
170	    }
171	  }, [])
172	
173	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
174	  // updateCard — rollback pattern (column changes ignored; use moveCard)
175	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
176	
177	  const updateCard = useCallback(async (id, data) => {
178	    // Strip column — column changes must go through moveCard
179	    const safeData = { ...data }
180	    delete safeData.column
181	
182	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref (not inside a lazy updater)
183	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
184	    applyCards(prev => {
185	      const next = {}
186	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
187	        next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...safeData } : c)
188	      }
189	      return next
190	    })
191	    beginOp()
192	
193	    try {
194	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, safeData)
195	      applyCards(prev => {
196	        const next = {}
197	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
198	          next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? updated : c)
199	        }
200	        return next
201	      })
202	      return updated
203	    } catch (err) {
204	      applyCards(rollback)
205	      throw err
206	    } finally {
207	      endOp()
208	    }
209	  }, [])
210	
211	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	  // deleteCard — rollback pattern
213	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	
215	  const deleteCard = useCallback(async (id) => {
216	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
217	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
218	    applyCards(prev => {
219	      const next = {}
220	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
221	        next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
222	      }
223	      return next
224	    })
225	    beginOp()
226	
227	    try {
228	      await apiDeleteCard(id)
229	    } catch (err) {
230	      applyCards(rollback)
231	      throw err
232	    } finally {
233	      endOp()
234	    }
235	  }, [])
236	
237	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
238	  // moveCard — rollback pattern
239	  // targetColumn must be in API format ('ready' | 'in-progress' | 'done')
240	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
241	
242	  const moveCard = useCallback(async (id, targetColumn, position) => {
243	    const targetKey = columnToKey(targetColumn)
244	
245	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
246	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
247	    applyCards(prev => {
248	      let cardToMove = null
249	      const next = {}
250	
251	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
252	        const idx = col.findIndex(c => c.id === id)
253	        if (idx !== -1) {
254	          cardToMove = col[idx]
255	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
256	        } else {
257	          next[k] = col
258	        }
259	      }
260	
261	      if (!cardToMove) return prev
262	
263	      const movedCard = {
264	        ...cardToMove,
265	        column: targetColumn,
266	        ...(position !== undefined ? { position } : {}),
267	      }
268	      next[targetKey] = [...next[targetKey], movedCard]
269	        .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
270	      return next
271	    })
272	    beginOp()
273	
274	    try {
275	      const payload = { column: targetColumn }
276	      if (position !== undefined) payload.position = position
277	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, payload)
278	      const updatedKey = columnToKey(updated.column)
279	      applyCards(prev => {
280	        const next = {}
281	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
282	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
283	        }
284	        next[updatedKey] = [...next[updatedKey], updated]
285	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
286	        return next
287	      })
288	      return updated
289	    } catch (err) {
290	      applyCards(rollback)
291	      throw err
292	    } finally {
293	      endOp()
294	    }
295	  }, [])
296	
297	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
298	  // addComment — temp-ID optimistic pattern
299	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
300	
301	  const addComment = useCallback(async (cardId, data) => {
302	    const tempId = nextTempId()
303	    const optimisticComment = {
304	      id: tempId,
305	      card_id: cardId,
306	      author: data.author,
307	      content: data.content,
308	      created_at: Date.now(),
309	    }
310	
311	    applyCards(prev => {
312	      const next = {}
313	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
314	        next[k] = col.map(c =>
315	          c.id === cardId
316	            ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, optimisticComment] }
317	            : c
318	        )
319	      }
320	      return next
321	    })
322	    beginOp()
323	
324	    try {
325	      const comment = await createComment(cardId, data)
326	      applyCards(prev => {
327	        const next = {}
328	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
329	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
330	            c.id === cardId
331	              ? {
332	                  ...c,
333	                  comments: c.comments
334	                    .filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId)
335	                    .concat(comment),
336	                }
337	              : c
338	          )
339	        }
340	        return next
341	      })
342	      return comment
343	    } catch (err) {
344	      applyCards(prev => {
345	        const next = {}
346	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
347	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
348	            c.id === cardId
349	              ? { ...c, comments: c.comments.filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId) }
350	              : c
351	          )
352	        }
353	        return next
354	      })
355	      throw err
356	    } finally {
357	      endOp()
358	    }
359	  }, [])
360	
361	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
362	  // Public interface
363	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
364	
365	  return {
366	    cards,
367	    loading,
368	    error,
369	    createCard,
370	    updateCard,
371	    deleteCard,
372	    moveCard,
373	    addComment,
374	  }
375	}
376

1	/**
2	 * API client for the Kanban board REST API.
3	 * All functions use relative URLs so Vite's dev proxy (/api → localhost:3001)
4	 * and same-origin production serving both work without configuration.
5	 */
6	
7	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8	// Type definitions
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	
11	/**
12	 * @typedef {Object} Card
13	 * @property {string} id
14	 * @property {string} title
15	 * @property {string|null} assignee
16	 * @property {string} column - e.g. 'ready', 'in-progress', 'done'
17	 * @property {number} position
18	 * @property {string|null} description
19	 * @property {number} created_at - Unix ms timestamp
20	 * @property {Comment[]} comments
21	 */
22	
23	/**
24	 * @typedef {Object} Comment
25	 * @property {string} id
26	 * @property {string} card_id
27	 * @property {string} author
28	 * @property {string} content
29	 * @property {number} created_at
30	 */
31	
32	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	// ApiError
34	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	
36	export class ApiError extends Error {
37	  /**
38	   * @param {string} msg
39	   * @param {number} status  HTTP status code, or 0 for network-level failures
40	   * @param {*} data         Parsed error response body, or null
41	   */
42	  constructor(msg, status, data) {
43	    super(msg)
44	    this.name = 'ApiError'
45	    this.status = status
46	    this.data = data
47	  }
48	}
49	
50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	// Internal fetch helper
52	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	async function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {
55	  const headers = {}
56	  if (options.body !== undefined) headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
57	
58	  let response
59	  try {
60	    response = await fetch(path, { ...options, headers: { ...headers, ...options.headers } })
61	  } catch (err) {
62	    throw new ApiError(`Network error: ${err.message}`, 0, null)
63	  }
64	
65	  if (!response.ok) {
66	    let errorData = null
67	    try { errorData = await response.json() } catch { /* ignore */ }
68	    const message = errorData?.error ?? `HTTP error ${response.status}`
69	    throw new ApiError(message, response.status, errorData)
70	  }
71	
72	  if (response.status === 204 || response.headers.get('content-length') === '0') {
73	    return null
74	  }
75	
76	  try {
77	    return await response.json()
78	  } catch (err) {
79	    throw new ApiError(`Failed to parse response: ${err.message}`, response.status, null)
80	  }
81	}
82	
83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	// Public API functions
85	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	/**
88	 * Fetch all cards with their nested comments.
89	 * @returns {Promise<Card[]>}
90	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
91	 * @example
92	 * const cards = await fetchCards()
93	 * console.log(cards[0].title)
94	 */
95	export async function fetchCards() {
96	  return apiFetch('/api/cards')
97	}
98	
99	/**
100	 * Create a new card.
101	 * @param {{ title: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string }} data
102	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
103	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
104	 * @example
105	 * const card = await createCard({ title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice' })
106	 * console.log(card.id)
107	 */
108	export async function createCard(data) {
109	  return apiFetch('/api/cards', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
110	}
111	
112	/**
113	 * Partially update an existing card.
114	 * @param {string} id
115	 * @param {{ title?: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string, position?: number }} data
116	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
117	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
118	 * @example
119	 * const updated = await updateCard('abc-123', { column: 'done' })
120	 */
121	export async function updateCard(id, data) {
122	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
123	}
124	
125	/**
126	 * Delete a card and all its comments.
127	 * @param {string} id
128	 * @returns {Promise<null>}
129	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
130	 * @example
131	 * await deleteCard('abc-123')
132	 */
133	export async function deleteCard(id) {
134	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' })
135	}
136	
137	/**
138	 * Add a comment to a card.
139	 * @param {string} cardId
140	 * @param {{ author: string, content: string }} data
141	 * @returns {Promise<Comment>}
142	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
143	 * @example
144	 * const comment = await createComment('abc-123', { author: 'Bob', content: 'Looks good!' })
145	 */
146	export async function createComment(cardId, data) {
147	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${cardId}/comments`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
148	}
149

I now have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Here is the complete TDD implementation plan. --- # TDD Implementation Plan: `CreateCardForm` ## Overview of the 3 Subtasks - **Subtask 1**: Create `CreateCardForm.jsx`, `CreateCardForm.test.jsx`, and `CreateCardForm.css` - **Subtask 2**: Modify `Column.jsx` and `Column.test.jsx` to accept an optional `footer` prop - **Subtask 3**: Modify `Board.jsx` and `Board.test.jsx` to wire up `CreateCardForm` in the Ready column Each subtask follows strict TDD: write failing tests first, then write the minimum implementation to make them pass. --- ## Subtask 1: `CreateCardForm` — Tests then Implementation ### TDD Order Write all tests in `CreateCardForm.test.jsx` first. Run the suite — every test should fail with "cannot find module" or similar since the component does not exist. Then create `CreateCardForm.jsx` to make them pass group by group. ### `CreateCardForm.test.jsx` — Full Spec ``` File location: kanban/client/src/components/CreateCardForm.test.jsx Imports needed: import { render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' import CreateCardForm from './CreateCardForm.jsx' ``` #### Shared test fixture ```js // Re-use in every describe block via a local onSubmit vi.fn() // Default: resolves immediately const onSubmit = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined) beforeEach(() => { onSubmit.mockClear() }) ``` --- #### `describe('CreateCardForm — toggle')` | # | Test name | What to assert | |---|-----------|---------------| | 1 | `renders the add button in collapsed state by default` | `screen.getByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i })` is in the document | | 2 | `does not render the form in collapsed state` | `screen.queryByRole('form')` or `queryByLabelText(/title/i)` is null | | 3 | `clicking the add button reveals the form` | after `fireEvent.click(addBtn)`, `getByLabelText(/title/i)` is in document | | 4 | `clicking the add button hides the add button itself` | after click, `queryByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i })` is null | --- #### `describe('CreateCardForm — form fields')` | # | Test name | What to assert | |---|-----------|---------------| | 1 | `renders a Title input when expanded` | `getByLabelText(/title/i)` present, type `text` | | 2 | `renders an Assignee input when expanded` | `getByLabelText(/assignee/i)` present | | 3 | `Title input is controlled and updates on change` | type 'Hello', assert value is 'Hello' | | 4 | `Assignee input is controlled and updates on change` | type 'Alice', assert value is 'Alice' | | 5 | `renders an Add card submit button` | `getByRole('button', { name: /^add card$/i })` present | | 6 | `renders a Cancel button` | `getByRole('button', { name: /^cancel$/i })` present | --- #### `describe('CreateCardForm — validation')` | # Passes toggle group tests 1 and 2. **Step 2** — Toggle open: add `isOpen` state, reveal a `
` on button click with Title and Assignee `` elements (controlled), plus "Add card" and "Cancel" submit/cancel buttons. Passes all toggle and form-field tests. **Step 3** — Validation: in `handleSubmit`, check `title.trim() === ''` → set `validationError` state → render `

` with the message. Clear error on title change. Disable inputs and button during submission. Show "Adding…" on button. Passes submission tests. **Step 5** — Error handling: catch the rejection, store in `apiError` state, render it as `

`, re-enable inputs. Clear `apiError` on title change. Passes error tests. **Step 6** — Cancel and Escape: `handleClose` function resets title, assignee, validationError, apiError, sets `isOpen(false)`. Wire Cancel button to it. Add `useEffect` listening for `keydown` Escape → call `handleClose` when `isOpen` is true. Passes cancel/Escape tests. --- ### Exact `CreateCardForm.jsx` Component Structure ``` State: isOpen → boolean, default false title → string, default '' assignee → string, default '' isSubmitting → boolean, default false validationError → string | null, default null apiError → string | null, default null Derived: displayError = validationError ?? apiError (only one can be set at a time) handleToggleOpen() → setIsOpen(true) handleClose() → setIsOpen(false), setTitle(''), setAssignee(''), setValidationError(null), setApiError(null) handleTitleChange(e) → setTitle(e.target.value) if validationError: setValidationError(null) if apiError: setApiError(null) handleAssigneeChange(e) → setAssignee(e.target.value) handleSubmit(e) → e.preventDefault() if title.trim() === '': setValidationError('Title is required'); return setIsSubmitting(true); setApiError(null) try: await onSubmit({ title: title.trim(), assignee: assignee.trim() || null }) handleClose() ← resets everything AND collapses catch(err): setApiError(err.message) finally: setIsSubmitting(false) Escape key effect: useEffect(() => { if (!isOpen) return function onKey(e) { if (e.key === 'Escape') handleClose() } document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) }, [isOpen]) // Note: handleClose must be stable or included in deps; // since it only touches setters, define inline in effect or use useCallback Render when !isOpen:

Render when isOpen:

{displayError}

``` **Important implementation note on Escape and `handleClose` stability**: define the Escape listener inline using a `useCallback`-wrapped `handleClose` or simply reference the state setters directly inside the effect. The cleanest approach matching the CardModal pattern is to add `isOpen` as the sole dependency and define the reset inline: ```js useEffect(() => { if (!isOpen) return function onKey(e) { if (e.key !== 'Escape') return setIsOpen(false); setTitle(''); setAssignee('') setValidationError(null); setApiError(null) } document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) }, [isOpen]) ``` --- ### `CreateCardForm.css` — Class Names and Styling ``` .create-card-form - flex: 1 - background: #1a1a2e - color: #fff - border: none - border-radius: 4px - padding: 0.35rem 0.75rem - font-size: 0.875rem - cursor: pointer - :disabled → opacity: 0.6, cursor: not-allowed .create-card-form-cancel - If `isOpen` is `false`, the effect returns early and adds no listener, so there is no cleanup risk. Tests that press Escape while collapsed should simply have no effect. **`displayError` precedence**: Only one of `validationError` or `apiError` is ever set at a time because `handleSubmit` clears `validationError` before attempting the async call, and `handleTitleChange` clears both. The merge `validationError ?? apiError` is safe and keeps a single `role="alert"` element in the DOM at any moment. **Board test `region` count**: The existing test `renders three column regions` asserts `screen.getAllByRole('region')).toHaveLength(3)`. ` inside `CreateCardForm` does NOT need `role="region"` — use `aria-label` on the form only if tests query it, otherwise skip. The count of 3 regions must remain stable. Verify by checking that the new Board test does not accidentally inflate this count. **Existing `DEFAULT_STATE` already has `createCard: vi.fn()`**: The `Board.test.jsx` DEFAULT_STATE already includes `createCard`. This means existing Board tests continue to pass without modification to `DEFAULT_STATE`. The `beforeEach` resets mock state, so there is no contamination. **`waitFor` import in Board.test.jsx**: The file currently does not import `waitFor`. The new submission test requires it. Add it to the existing import line before running the new tests. --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-12/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-12/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-12/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-12/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-12/kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx`

We need a togglable form at the bottom of the Ready column that lets users type a title (required) and assignee (optional), then submit to create a card via `useBoard().createCard()`. getByLabelText(/title/i) present it('clicking the add button hides the add button itself') // after click → queryByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i }) → null }) describe('CreateCardForm — form fields', () => { // Open form before each: fireEvent.click(getByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i })) it('renders a Title input when expanded') // getByLabelText(/title/i) it('renders an Assignee input when expanded') // getByLabelText(/assignee/i) it('Title input is controlled') // type → check value it('Assignee input is controlled') // type → check value it('renders an Add card submit button') // getByRole('button', { name: /^add card$/i }) it('renders a Cancel button') // getByRole('button', { name: /^cancel$/i }) }) describe('CreateCardForm — validation', () => { it('shows inline error when submitted with empty title') // click submit with empty title → getByRole('alert') has /title is required/i it('shows inline error when submitted with whitespace-only title') // set title=' starts typing in title') // trigger error, then fireEvent.change on title → queryByRole('alert') null }) describe('CreateCardForm — submission', () => { it('calls onSubmit with trimmed title and null assignee when assignee is empty') // fill title='Bug fix ', assignee='', submit // → onSubmit called with { title: 'Bug fix', assignee: null } it('calls onSubmit with trimmed title and trimmed assignee when both filled') // → { title: 'Bug fix', assignee: 'Alice' } it('calls onSubmit with null assignee when assignee is whitespace-only') // assignee=' ' → { assignee: null } it('submit button shows "Adding…" while onSubmit is pending') // onSubmit = new Promise(() => {}), click submit → button text "Adding…" it('submit button is disabled while onSubmit is pending') // same → button disabled it('title and assignee inputs are disabled while onSubmit is pending') // same → both inputs disabled it('clears both fields and collapses form after successful submission') // await waitFor → title input gone, add button visible again }) describe('CreateCardForm — error handling', () => { it('shows API error message when onSubmit rejects') // onSubmit.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Server error')) // submit → await waitFor → getByRole('alert') has "Server error" it('keeps form open after onSubmit rejects') // after rejection → title input still present it('re-enables inputs after onSubmit rejects') // after rejection → title input not disabled it('re-enables submit button after onSubmit rejects') it('clears API error when user starts typing after a failed submit') // trigger error, type in title → queryByRole('alert') null }) describe('CreateCardForm — cancel and Escape', () => { it('Cancel button collapses form and shows add button') it('Cancel button resets title field') // expand, type, cancel, re-expand → value '' it('Cancel button resets assignee field') it('Escape key collapses form') // fireEvent.keyDown(document.body, { key: 'Escape' }) → form gone field') // expand, type, cancel, re-expand → value '' it('Cancel button resets assignee field') it('Escape key collapses form') // fireEvent.keyDown(document.body, { key: 'Escape' }) → form gone it('Escape key resets title and assignee fields') it('Escape key does nothing when form is not open') }) ``` ### Step 2 — Implement CreateCardForm.jsx **State:** ``` isOpen → boolean, false title → string, '' assignee → string, '' isSubmitting → boolean, false validationError → string | null, null apiError → string | null, null ``` **Key handlers:** ```js handleClose() → setIsOpen(false), reset all fields and errors handleTitleChange(e) → setTitle(e.target.value) clear validationError and apiError handleSubmit(e) → e.preventDefault() if !title.trim(): setValidationError('Title is required'); return setIsSubmitting(true); setApiError(null) try: await onSubmit({ title: title.trim(), assignee: assignee.trim() || null }) handleClose() catch(err): setApiError(err.message) finally: setIsSubmitting(false) // Escape key effect useEffect(() => { if (!isOpen) return function onKey(e) { if (e.key !== 'Escape') return setIsOpen(false); setTitle(''); setAssignee(''); setValidationError(null); setApiError(null) } document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) }, [isOpen]) ``` **Render when `!isOpen`:** ```jsx
``` **Render when `isOpen`:** ```jsx
setAssignee(e.target.value)} disabled={isSubmitting} /> {(validationError || apiError) && (

{validationError ?? apiError}

)}
``` ### Step 3 — Create CreateCardForm.css CSS classes (follow existing BEM-like naming from CardTile.css, Column.css): - `.create-card-form` — padding-top: 0.5rem - `.create-card-form-toggle` — full-width, dashed border, muted color, cursor pointer; hover darkens - `.create-card-form-body` — flex column, gap 0.4rem - `.create-card-form-body label` — small font, semi-bold - `.create-card-form-body input` — full-width, border, border-radius 4px; focus ring - `.create-card-form-error` — red text (#c0392b), small font - `.create-card-form-actions` — flex row, gap 0.4rem - `.create-card-form-submit` — dark background (#1a1a2e), white text, border-radius 4px; disabled → opacity 0.6 - `.create-card-form-cancel` — transparent bg, bordered, muted; disabled → opacity 0.6 --- ## Subtask 2: Column footer prop (TDD) ### Step 1 — Add failing tests to Column.test.jsx Add at end of the existing `describe('Column', ...)` block: ```js it('renders the footer prop below the cards area when provided', () => { render( Footer content} /> ) expect(screen.getByTestId('col-footer')).toBeInTheDocument() }) it('renders nothing extra when footer prop is not provided', () => { const { container } = render() const section = container.querySelector('.column') expect(section.children).toHaveLength(2) // header + column-cards only }) ``` ### Step 2 — Update Column.jsx Two changes: destructure `footer`, render `{footer}` after `.column-cards`: ```diff -export default function Column({ title, cards, onCardClick }) { +export default function Column({ title, cards, onCardClick, footer }) { return (
...
...
+ {footer}
) } ``` --- ## Subtask 3: Board integration (TDD) ### Step 1 — Add failing tests to Board.test.jsx Add `waitFor` to the existing import line: ```diff -import { render, screen, fireEvent, within } from '@testing-library/react' +import { render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor, within } from '@testing-library/react' ``` Add a new describe block at the end: ```js describe('Board — CreateCardForm integration', () => { it('renders the "+ Add card" button in the Ready column', () => { render() const readyRegion = screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Ready' }) expect(within(readyRegion).getByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i })).toBeInTheDocument() }) it('does not render the "+ Add card" button in the In Progress column', () => { render() const ipRegion = screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'In Progress' }) expect(within(ipRegion).queryByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i })).toBeNull() }) it('does not render the "+ Add card" button in the Done column', () => { render() const doneRegion = screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Done' }) expect(within(doneRegion).queryByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i })).toBeNull() }) it('calls createCard from useBoard when the form is submitted', async () => { const createCard = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined) useBoard.mockReturnValue({ ...DEFAULT_STATE, createCard }) render() const readyRegion = screen.getByRole('region', { name: 'Ready' }) fireEvent.click(within(readyRegion).getByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i })) fireEvent.change(screen.getByLabelText(/title/i), { target: { value: 'My new card' } }) fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button', { name: /^add card$/i })) await waitFor(() => expect(createCard).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ title: 'My new card', assignee: null }) ) }) }) ``` > Note: `DEFAULT_STATE` already includes `createCard: vi.fn()` so existing Board tests continue to pass unchanged. > Note: The existing `renders three column regions` test asserts `toHaveLength(3)`. The `
` inside CreateCardForm does NOT use `role="region"` — it will not inflate the count. ### Step 2 — Update Board.jsx Four targeted changes: ```diff import { useState } from 'react' import { useBoard } from '../../hooks/useBoard.js' import Column from './Column.jsx' import CardModal from './CardModal.jsx' +import CreateCardForm from '../CreateCardForm.jsx' import './Board.css' export default function Board() { - const { cards, loading, error, updateCard, deleteCard } = useBoard() + const { cards, loading, error, createCard, updateCard, deleteCard } = useBoard() ... return (
- setSelectedCardId(card.id)} /> + setSelectedCardId(card.id)} + footer={} + /> ...
) } ``` --- ## Execution Order 1. Write `CreateCardForm.test.jsx` (all tests — all fail with module not found) 2. Write `CreateCardForm.jsx` (pass toggle + field + validation + submission + error + cancel tests) 3. Write `CreateCardForm.css` (styles only, no test impact) 4. Add Column footer tests to `Column.test.jsx` (fail) 5. Update `Column.jsx` (pass column footer tests) 6. Add Board integration tests to `Board.test.jsx` (fail) 7. Update `Board.jsx` (pass board integration tests) 8. Run full test suite — all green --- ## Verification Run the test suite from the client directory: ```bash cd kanban/client && npm test ``` Key checks: - All `CreateCardForm` describe blocks pass (toggle, fields, validation, submission, errors, cancel/Escape) - Existing Column tests still pass + 2 new footer tests pass - Existing Board tests still pass (especially `renders three column regions` asserting length 3) + 4 new integration tests pass - Manual smoke test: `npm run dev` → board loads → Ready column shows "+ Add card" → click → form appears → fill title → submit → card appears optimistically → form collapses

content.text:
Here's a summary of the plan I've written:

## Plan: CreateCardForm Component

### What we're building
A self-contained inline form at the bottom of the **Ready column** for creating new cards, following TDD with strict Red→Green→Refactor cycles.

### Files

| Action | File |
|--------|------|
| **Create** | `client/src/components/CreateCardForm.test.jsx` (all tests first) |
| **Create** | `client/src/components/CreateCardForm.jsx` |
| **Create** | `client/src/components/CreateCardForm.css` |
| **Modify** | `Board/Column.jsx` + `Column.test.jsx` (add optional `footer` prop) |
| **Modify** | `Board/Board.jsx` + `Board.test.jsx` (wire up the form) |

### Architecture
- `CreateCardForm` is **self-contained**: manages its own toggle (collapsed/expanded), validation, loading state, and error handling
- `Column` gets a generic `footer` prop (a React node rendered below the cards list)
- `Board` passes `<CreateCardForm onSubmit={createCard} />` as the `footer` only to the Ready column

### TDD Order (3 subtasks)
1. **All `CreateCardForm` tests** → implement the component (toggle, fields, validation, submission, errors, cancel, Escape)
2. **Column footer tests** → add `footer` prop to `Column.jsx`  
3. **Board integration tests** → update `Board.jsx` to wire it all together

### Key behaviors tested
- Toggle: `+ Add card` button → reveals form; collapse on cancel/Escape/success
- Validation: empty/whitespace title shows inline alert, blocks submission
- Submission: calls `onSubmit({ title: trimmed, assignee: null | trimmed })`, loading state disables inputs
- Success: form clears and collapses; Error: form stays open, re-enables, shows API error message
- Board: `+ Add card` appears only in Ready column; `createCard` from `useBoard` is called on submit

1	import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
2	import {
3	  fetchCards,
4	  createCard as apiCreateCard,
5	  updateCard as apiUpdateCard,
6	  deleteCard as apiDeleteCard,
7	  createComment,
8	} from '../api/client.js'
9	
10	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
11	// Module-level helpers
12	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
13	
14	/**
15	 * Map API column value → state key.
16	 * 'in-progress' (hyphen) → 'in_progress' (underscore); others pass through.
17	 * @param {string} col
18	 * @returns {string}
19	 */
20	export function columnToKey(col) {
21	  return col === 'in-progress' ? 'in_progress' : col
22	}
23	
24	/**
25	 * Group a flat array of cards into the board state shape, sorted by position.
26	 * @param {import('../api/client.js').Card[]} cards
27	 * @returns {{ ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }}
28	 */
29	function groupCards(cards) {
30	  const board = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
31	  for (const card of cards) {
32	    const key = columnToKey(card.column)
33	    if (key in board) board[key].push(card)
34	  }
35	  for (const key of Object.keys(board)) {
36	    board[key].sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
37	  }
38	  return board
39	}
40	
41	const EMPTY_BOARD = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
42	
43	// Temp-ID counter for optimistic records (module-level so it's stable across renders)
44	let _tempId = 0
45	function nextTempId() {
46	  return '__temp_' + (++_tempId)
47	}
48	
49	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	// useBoard hook
51	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	
53	/**
54	 * Manages board state: cards grouped by column, loading, and error.
55	 *
56	 * Column convention
57	 *   - State keys:  ready | in_progress | done  (underscore)
58	 *   - API values:  ready | in-progress  | done  (hyphen for in-progress)
59	 *   - All hook functions that accept a column name expect the API format.
60	 *   - Each card's .column field retains the API format.
61	 *
62	 * @returns {{
63	 *   cards: { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] },
64	 *   loading: boolean,
65	 *   error: string|null,
66	 *   createCard: (data: object) => Promise<Card>,
67	 *   updateCard: (id: string, data: object) => Promise<Card>,
68	 *   deleteCard: (id: string) => Promise<void>,
69	 *   moveCard: (id: string, targetColumn: string, position?: number) => Promise<Card>,
70	 *   addComment: (cardId: string, data: object) => Promise<Comment>,
71	 * }}
72	 */
73	export function useBoard() {
74	  const [cards, setCards] = useState(EMPTY_BOARD)
75	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
76	  const [error, setError] = useState(null)
77	
78	  // Counter for concurrent in-flight operations so loading stays true
79	  // until the LAST operation finishes.
80	  const pendingRef = useRef(0)
81	
82	  // Ref that always holds the latest cards state so we can read it
83	  // synchronously (functional updaters are called lazily by React, which
84	  // would leave a rollback variable null if the API rejects as a microtask
85	  // before the updater runs).
86	  const cardsRef = useRef(EMPTY_BOARD)
87	
88	  /**
89	   * Apply a state update eagerly: compute the next value synchronously from
90	   * cardsRef.current, update the ref, then call setCards with the plain
91	   * computed value (not a function) so React picks it up on the next render.
92	   */
93	  function applyCards(updater) {
94	    const next = typeof updater === 'function' ? updater(cardsRef.current) : updater
95	    cardsRef.current = next
96	    setCards(next)
97	  }
98	
99	  function beginOp() { pendingRef.current++; setLoading(true) }
100	  function endOp()   { if (--pendingRef.current === 0) setLoading(false) }
101	
102	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	  // Initial data load
104	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	
106	  useEffect(() => {
107	    let cancelled = false
108	    setError(null)
109	    beginOp()
110	
111	    fetchCards()
112	      .then(data => {
113	        if (cancelled) return
114	        applyCards(groupCards(data))
115	        endOp()
116	      })
117	      .catch(err => {
118	        if (cancelled) return
119	        setError(err.message)
120	        endOp()
121	      })
122	
123	    return () => { cancelled = true }
124	  }, [])
125	
126	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	  // createCard — temp-ID optimistic pattern
128	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	
130	  const createCard = useCallback(async (data) => {
131	    const tempId = nextTempId()
132	    const col = data.column ?? 'ready'
133	    const key = columnToKey(col)
134	
135	    const optimisticCard = {
136	      id: tempId,
137	      title: data.title,
138	      assignee: data.assignee ?? null,
139	      column: col,
140	      position: Infinity,
141	      description: data.description ?? null,
142	      created_at: Date.now(),
143	      comments: [],
144	    }
145	
146	    applyCards(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: [...prev[key], optimisticCard] }))
147	    beginOp()
148	
149	    try {
150	      const created = await apiCreateCard(data)
151	      const createdKey = columnToKey(created.column)
152	      applyCards(prev => {
153	        const next = { ...prev }
154	        // Remove the temp card from whichever column it was placed in
155	        next[key] = next[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId)
156	        // Insert the server card into the column the server assigned
157	        next[createdKey] = [...next[createdKey], created]
158	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
159	        return next
160	      })
161	      return created
162	    } catch (err) {
163	      applyCards(prev => ({
164	        ...prev,
165	        [key]: prev[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId),
166	      }))
167	      throw err
168	    } finally {
169	      endOp()
170	    }
171	  }, [])
172	
173	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
174	  // updateCard — rollback pattern (column changes ignored; use moveCard)
175	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
176	
177	  const updateCard = useCallback(async (id, data) => {
178	    // Strip column — column changes must go through moveCard
179	    const safeData = { ...data }
180	    delete safeData.column
181	
182	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref (not inside a lazy updater)
183	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
184	    applyCards(prev => {
185	      const next = {}
186	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
187	        next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...safeData } : c)
188	      }
189	      return next
190	    })
191	    beginOp()
192	
193	    try {
194	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, safeData)
195	      applyCards(prev => {
196	        const next = {}
197	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
198	          next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? updated : c)
199	        }
200	        return next
201	      })
202	      return updated
203	    } catch (err) {
204	      applyCards(rollback)
205	      throw err
206	    } finally {
207	      endOp()
208	    }
209	  }, [])
210	
211	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
212	  // deleteCard — rollback pattern
213	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	
215	  const deleteCard = useCallback(async (id) => {
216	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
217	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
218	    applyCards(prev => {
219	      const next = {}
220	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
221	        next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
222	      }
223	      return next
224	    })
225	    beginOp()
226	
227	    try {
228	      await apiDeleteCard(id)
229	    } catch (err) {
230	      applyCards(rollback)
231	      throw err
232	    } finally {
233	      endOp()
234	    }
235	  }, [])
236	
237	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
238	  // moveCard — rollback pattern
239	  // targetColumn must be in API format ('ready' | 'in-progress' | 'done')
240	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
241	
242	  const moveCard = useCallback(async (id, targetColumn, position) => {
243	    const targetKey = columnToKey(targetColumn)
244	
245	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
246	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
247	    applyCards(prev => {
248	      let cardToMove = null
249	      const next = {}
250	
251	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
252	        const idx = col.findIndex(c => c.id === id)
253	        if (idx !== -1) {
254	          cardToMove = col[idx]
255	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
256	        } else {
257	          next[k] = col
258	        }
259	      }
260	
261	      if (!cardToMove) return prev
262	
263	      const movedCard = {
264	        ...cardToMove,
265	        column: targetColumn,
266	        ...(position !== undefined ? { position } : {}),
267	      }
268	      next[targetKey] = [...next[targetKey], movedCard]
269	        .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
270	      return next
271	    })
272	    beginOp()
273	
274	    try {
275	      const payload = { column: targetColumn }
276	      if (position !== undefined) payload.position = position
277	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, payload)
278	      const updatedKey = columnToKey(updated.column)
279	      applyCards(prev => {
280	        const next = {}
281	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
282	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
283	        }
284	        next[updatedKey] = [...next[updatedKey], updated]
285	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
286	        return next
287	      })
288	      return updated
289	    } catch (err) {
290	      applyCards(rollback)
291	      throw err
292	    } finally {
293	      endOp()
294	    }
295	  }, [])
296	
297	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
298	  // addComment — temp-ID optimistic pattern
299	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
300	
301	  const addComment = useCallback(async (cardId, data) => {
302	    const tempId = nextTempId()
303	    const optimisticComment = {
304	      id: tempId,
305	      card_id: cardId,
306	      author: data.author,
307	      content: data.content,
308	      created_at: Date.now(),
309	    }
310	
311	    applyCards(prev => {
312	      const next = {}
313	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
314	        next[k] = col.map(c =>
315	          c.id === cardId
316	            ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, optimisticComment] }
317	            : c
318	        )
319	      }
320	      return next
321	    })
322	    beginOp()
323	
324	    try {
325	      const comment = await createComment(cardId, data)
326	      applyCards(prev => {
327	        const next = {}
328	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
329	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
330	            c.id === cardId
331	              ? {
332	                  ...c,
333	                  comments: c.comments
334	                    .filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId)
335	                    .concat(comment),
336	                }
337	              : c
338	          )
339	        }
340	        return next
341	      })
342	      return comment
343	    } catch (err) {
344	      applyCards(prev => {
345	        const next = {}
346	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
347	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
348	            c.id === cardId
349	              ? { ...c, comments: c.comments.filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId) }
350	              : c
351	          )
352	        }
353	        return next
354	      })
355	      throw err
356	    } finally {
357	      endOp()
358	    }
359	  }, [])
360	
361	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
362	  // Public interface
363	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
364	
365	  return {
366	    cards,
367	    loading,
368	    error,
369	    createCard,
370	    updateCard,
371	    deleteCard,
372	    moveCard,
373	    addComment,
374	  }
375	}

content.thinking:
Now I can see the `useBoard.js` code. Critical finding on line 132:

```js
const col = data.column ?? 'ready'
```

So `column` defaults to `'ready'` if not provided! This means the form does NOT need to explicitly pass `column: 'ready'`. The optimistic update will default to the ready column.

So the plan is actually correct on this point - we don't need to include `column: 'ready'` in the submission payload. The hook handles this automatically.

Now let me re-evaluate my list of issues:


1. ~~Add `column: 'ready'` to submission payload~~ - NOT needed. The hook defaults to 'ready' automatically.

2. **Auto-focus for title input** - Still a valid issue. When the form opens, the title should auto-focus. This is a good UX pattern (matches CardModal) and should be included.

3. **Test structure clarity** - Still needed. The plan mentions "Open form before each" as a comment but doesn't show explicit `beforeEach` setup.

4. **Missing test: focuses title input when form opens** - Should be added.

Now I'm looking at the `createCard` function in the hook to understand the full flow...

Examining the async card creation logic...

So the default column assignment is working correctly—when we call `createCard` without specifying a column, it defaults to 'ready' and the optimistic update places the card in the right spot. The plan's approach is sound.

The real gaps are around the auto-focus behavior. The `CardModal` already demonstrates this pattern with a ref and effect that focuses the title input when editing starts, and we should apply the same approach here when the form initially opens.

For the tests, I need to be more explicit about setup. Rather than relying on comments to indicate the form should be open, each test block that needs it should have a clear setup step so tests don't accidentally run against a closed form.

On the mock management side, I'm seeing a potential issue with how `onSubmit` is being handled across different test blocks. The approach of creating `vi.fn()` fresh in each test works well for isolated tests, but when multiple tests share the same rendered component through `beforeEach`, I need to be consistent about whether I'm creating a new mock per test or reusing one across the block.

For the error handling and pending state tests specifically, each one needs its own mock behavior—rejecting, pending, resolving differently—so they shouldn't all share a single `onSubmit` instance. The submission tests need to be clearer about whether they're creating fresh mocks or relying on a shared one. The `handleClose` function should be the single source of truth for closing the form—resetting state and setting `isOpen` to false—and both the Cancel button and the Escape key effect should call it rather than duplicating the logic inline. I need to make sure the plan explicitly states this so the implementation stays consistent. Writing the escape key handler... When `isOpen` is `false`, the effect returns early without setting up a listener or cleanup, but React still runs the previous cleanup function when the dependency changes, so the listener gets properly removed. The `handleClose` function can reuse the same state setters to reset the form, and both the Cancel button and the Escape key handler will call this same function.

For the error display, I'm computing `displayError` as a derived value directly in the component body rather than storing it in state, using the nullish coalescing operator to pick whichever error is set.

Now I need to identify the actual gaps in the plan: the auto-focus behavior is missing entirely, the test structure could be clearer about when the form needs to be opened, and I should confirm that `handleClose` is used consistently for both the Escape key and Cancel button to reset the state. The `column: 'ready'` default isn't an issue since the hook already handles that. Let me revise the plan to address these points.

content.thinking:
Now let me evaluate the plan carefully against what I've learned from reading `useBoard.js`:

## Issues Found

### Issue 1: `column` in submission payload (RESOLVED — not an issue)
From `useBoard.js` line 132: `const col = data.column ?? 'ready'`

The hook defaults `column` to `'ready'` when not provided. So NOT passing `column: 'ready'` is fine. The Board test expectation `{ title: 'My new card', assignee: null }` is correct.

### Issue 2: Auto-focus for title input (REAL ISSUE)


The plan doesn't include auto-focus for the title input when the form opens. This is missing from the component implementation and test suite, but it's a UX feature that aligns with the task requirements around graceful form handling and the subtask about smooth transitions for form appearance.

### Issue 3: Test structure ambiguity (REAL ISSUE)
The test organization needs clarification on how the test suites are structured and what each one covers.

### Issue 4: Missing `afterEach` / mock cleanup
The plan mentions a shared `onSubmit` mock but doesn't specify how to handle tests that need different mock behaviors or how cleanup between tests should work.

### Issue 5: `handleClose` vs inline setters in Escape effect
There's an inconsistency between using `handleClose()` for resetting state versus inline setters in the Escape effect. The safest approach would be to keep inline setters in the effect to avoid dependency array complications, since `handleClose` as a regular function would need to be included in dependencies and would be recreated each render.

### Issue 6: `handleSubmit` validation error handling
Looking at the flow, when a user submits with an empty title, `validationError` gets set. Then when they type a valid title, the error clears. So on the next submit attempt with valid input, `validationError` is already null—the plan handles this correctly by clearing the error when the user starts typing rather than in `handleSubmit` itself.

### Issue 7: Edge case with whitespace and focus
What if the user submits with spaces, sees the validation error, but then doesn't actually type anything new—just keeps the focus in the field? The error would persist since `handleTitleChange` only fires on actual input changes.

### Issue 8: The "Cancel button resets title field" test
The test expands the form, types something, cancels, then re-expands to verify the title field is empty again.

### Issue 9: Missing test for initial error state
When the form first opens, no error alerts should display since `validationError` and `apiError` start as null. This is implied by the implementation but not explicitly tested—though it's probably not worth adding since it's a straightforward edge case.

## Summary of Real Issues

The main gaps I'm finding are: the title input should auto-focus when the form opens but this is missing from both the implementation and tests, and the test plan uses pseudo-code comments without showing the actual test structure (render, open, action, assert), which could lead to inconsistent implementations.

There's also a minor inconsistency between how `handleClose` and the Escape key effect work that should be clarified.

I'll add the auto-focus feature using a ref and effect that triggers when `isOpen` changes, include a test for it, show a complete example of how form-open tests should be structured, and clarify the close handler consistency in the implementation spec.

content.text:
I found three real issues to fix in the plan:

1. **Auto-focus missing**: When the form opens, the title input should auto-focus (matches the `CardModal.jsx` pattern for all inline editing). Neither the component spec nor the test suite mentions this.

2. **Test structure ambiguity**: Describe blocks that need the form open (form fields, validation, submission, errors, cancel) show only comment hints like `// Open form before each: fireEvent.click(...)`. The implementing LLM needs explicit patterns — each test must call `render()`, the `vi.fn()` per-test mock pattern must be shown for tests with different async behaviors (pending, rejecting, resolving).

3. **Escape effect vs `handleClose` inconsistency**: The Escape effect uses inline setters while Cancel uses `handleClose`. Since `handleClose` is a plain function defined inside the component (recreated every render), referencing it in the `useEffect` dep array is problematic. The plan must resolve this clearly by inlining the setters in both places, or by defining `handleClose` consistently.

# Plan: CreateCardForm Component (Task 12) ## Context This task adds inline card creation to the Ready column of the Kanban board. Currently, cards can only be created via the API directly — there is no UI for it. We need a togglable form at the bottom of the Ready column that lets users type a title (required) and assignee (optional), then submit to create a card via `useBoard().createCard()`. The implementation follows the same async-with-loading-state pattern used by `CardModal.jsx` and integrates via a new optional `footer` prop on `Column`. --- ## Architecture - **New**: `client/src/components/CreateCardForm.jsx` — self-contained (toggle + form + validation + async submission) - **New**: `client/src/components/CreateCardForm.test.jsx` — full test suite - **New**: `client/src/components/CreateCardForm.css` — plain CSS matching existing patterns - **Modify**: `client/src/components/Board/Column.jsx` — add optional `footer` prop rendered below `.column-cards` - **Modify**: `client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` — add 2 footer tests - **Modify**: `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` — destructure `createCard`, pass `` as `footer` to Ready column only - **Modify**: `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` — add `waitFor` import + 4 integration tests --- ## Subtask 1: CreateCardForm component (TDD) ### Step 1 — Write all tests first (all fail) Create `client/src/components/CreateCardForm.test.jsx`: ```jsx import { render, screen, fireEvent, waitFor } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest' import CreateCardForm from './CreateCardForm.jsx' describe('CreateCardForm — toggle', () => { it('renders the add button in collapsed state by default') // screen.getByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i }) it('does not render the form in collapsed state') // screen.queryByLabelText(/title/i) → null it('clicking the add button reveals the form') // fireEvent.click(addBtn) → getByLabelText(/title/i) present it('clicking the add button hides the add button itself') // after click → queryByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i }) → null }) describe('CreateCardForm — form fields', () => { // Open form before each: fireEvent.click(getByRole('button', { name: /\+ add card/i })) it('renders a Title input when expanded') // getByLabelText(/title/i) it('renders an Assignee input when expanded') // getByLabelText(/assignee/i) it('Title input is controlled') // type → check value it('Assignee input is controlled') // type → check value it('renders an Add card submit button') // getByRole('button', { name: /^add card$/i }) it('renders a Cancel button') // getByRole('button', { name: /^cancel$/i }) }) describe('CreateCardForm — validation', () => { it('shows inline error when submitted with empty title') // click submit with empty title → getByRole('alert') has /title is required/i it('shows inline error when submitted with whitespace-only title') // set title=' ', submit → alert /title is required/i it('does not call onSubmit when title is empty') // submit with empty title → expect(onSubmit).not.toHaveBeenCalled() it('clears validation error when user starts typing in title') // trigger error, then fireEvent.change on title → queryByRole('alert') null }) describe('CreateCardForm — submission', () => { it('calls onSubmit with trimmed title and null assignee when assignee is empty') // fill title='Bug fix ', assignee='', submit // → onSubmit called with { title: 'Bug fix', assignee: null } it('calls onSubmit with trimmed title and trimmed assignee when both filled') // → { title: 'Bug fix', assignee: 'Alice' } it('calls onSubmit with null assignee when assignee is whitespace-only') // assignee=' ' → { assignee: null } it('submit button shows "Adding…" while onSubmit is pending') // onSubmit = new Promise(() => {}), click submit → button text "Adding…" it('submit button is disabled while onSubmit is pending') // same → button disabled it('title and assignee inputs are disabled while onSubmit is pending') // same → both inputs disabled it('clears both fields and collapses form after successful submission') // await waitFor → title input gone, add button visible again }) describe('CreateCardForm — error handling', () => { it('shows API error message when onSubmit rejects') // onSubmit.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Server error')) // submit → await waitFor → getByRole('alert') has "Server error" it('keeps form open after onSubmit rejects') // after rejection → title input still present it('re-enables inputs after onSubmit rejects') // after rejection → title input not disabled it('re-enables submit button after onSubmit rejects') it('clears API error when user starts typing after a failed submit') // trigger error, type in title → queryByRole('alert') null }) describe('CreateCardForm — cancel and Escape', () => { it('Cancel button collapses form and shows add button') it('Cancel button resets title field') // expand, type, cancel, re-expand → value '' it('Cancel button resets assignee field') it('Escape key collapses form') // fireEvent.keyDown(document.body, { key: 'Escape' }) → form gone it('Escape key resets title and assignee fields') it('Escape key does nothing when form is not open') }) ``` ### Step 2 — Implement CreateCardForm.jsx **State:** ``` isOpen → boolean, false title → string, '' assignee → string, '' isSubmitting → boolean, false validationError → string | null, null apiError → string | null, null ``` **Key handlers:** ```js handleClose() → setIsOpen(false), reset all fields and errors handleTitleChange(e) → setTitle(e.target.value) clear validationError and apiError handleSubmit(e) → e.preventDefault() if !title.trim(): setValidationError('Title is required'); return setIsSubmitting(true); setApiError(null) try: await onSubmit({ title: title.trim(), assignee: assignee.trim() || null }) handleClose() catch(err): setApiError(err.message) finally: setIsSubmitting(false) // Escape key effect useEffect(() => { if (!isOpen) return function onKey(e) { if (e.key !== 'Escape') return setIsOpen(false); setTitle(''); setAssignee(''); setValidationError(null); setApiError(null) } document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey) return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey) }, [isOpen]) ``` **Render when `!isOpen`:** content.text:
The plan has been updated with three corrections:

1. **Auto-focus added**: The component now includes `titleInputRef` + a `useEffect` that focuses the title input when `isOpen` becomes `true` (matching the `CardModal.jsx` pattern). A corresponding test `'focuses the title input when the form is opened'` was added to the toggle describe block.

2. **Test structure made explicit**: Every test now shows the full code — `render()`, mock creation, "open form" click, actions, and assertions. Helper functions (`renderOpen`, `renderOpenAndFill`, `renderOpenAndSubmit`) are defined per-describe-block to avoid state sharing across tests while keeping code concise. All tests that need different async behavior (pending, rejecting) create their own `vi.fn()` inline.

3. **`handleClose` vs Escape effect clarified**: The component spec now explicitly documents that the Escape effect duplicates the reset logic inline (not calling `handleClose`) to keep `[isOpen]` as its sole dependency — same pattern as `CardModal.jsx`. `handleClose` is used by the Cancel button and after successful submission. A comment in the code explains the design decision.

Additionally confirmed (from reading `useBoard.js` line 132): `const col = data.column ?? 'ready'` — the hook already defaults to `'ready'`, so the form payload `{ title, assignee }` is correct and the Board test expectation is accurate.

## Issue Analysis

### 1. `handleSubmit` async + `setIsSubmitting(false)` after `handleClose()`

Looking at the `handleSubmit` implementation:

```js
async function handleSubmit(e) {
  e.preventDefault()
  if (!title.trim()) {
    setValidationError('Title is required')
    return
  }
  setIsSubmitting(true)
  setApiError(null)
  try {
    await onSubmit({ title: title.trim(), assignee: assignee.trim() || null })
    handleClose()   // sets isOpen=false, title='', assignee='', etc.
  } catch (err) {
    setApiError(err.message)
  } finally {
    setIsSubmitting(false)   // ← runs AFTER handleClose()
  }
}
```

On the success path, `handleClose()` resets the form state and closes the modal, then the `finally` block sets `isSubmitting` to false. But since `handleClose()` already clears the form, the `setIsSubmitting(false)` call happens after the modal is already closed, which could cause a state update on an unmounted component if the modal closes immediately.

Actually, the component itself stays mounted — it just toggles between showing the form and the toggle button. So `setIsSubmitting(false)` in the `finally` block is safe even after `handleClose()` runs, since the component is still in the DOM and React batches the updates together. The auto-focus effect triggers whenever `isOpen` changes, but since the form only renders when `isOpen` is true, the ref will be null when closing anyway—so the early condition check prevents any issues. ✓

Now I'm thinking through the test timing: `fireEvent.click` doesn't automatically wrap in `act()`, but the state update from `handleOpen` happens synchronously, so React batches it and the component re-renders. The `useEffect` should fire after that, focusing the input.

### 9. The `validationError` is never cleared when submitting successfully

Looking at `handleSubmit`, it clears `apiError` when starting submission but doesn't clear `validationError`, which means if a validation error existed from a previous attempt, it would persist even after a successful submission.

The `handleTitleChange` function clears the error on any change, so as soon as they type something valid, it disappears. ✓

5	This task adds inline card creation to the Ready column of the Kanban board. Currently, cards can only be created via the API directly — there is no UI for it. We need a togglable form at the bottom of the Ready column that lets users type a title (required) and assignee (optional), then submit to create a card via `useBoard().createCard()`.

332	### Step 2 — Implement CreateCardForm.jsx
333	
334	```jsx
335	import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from 'react'
336	import './CreateCardForm.css'
337	
338	export default function CreateCardForm({ onSubmit }) {
339	  const [isOpen, setIsOpen]                     = useState(false)
340	  const [title, setTitle]                       = useState('')
341	  const [assignee, setAssignee]                 = useState('')
342	  const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting]         = useState(false)
343	  const [validationError, setValidationError]   = useState(null)
344	  const [apiError, setApiError]                 = useState(null)
345	
346	  const titleInputRef = useRef(null)
347	
348	  // Auto-focus title input when form opens (matches CardModal pattern)
349	  useEffect(() => {
350	    if (isOpen && titleInputRef.current) titleInputRef.current.focus()
351	  }, [isOpen])
352	
353	  // Escape key closes and resets the form (only when open)
354	  // State setters are stable React guarantees, so [isOpen] is the only dep needed.
355	  useEffect(() => {
356	    if (!isOpen) return
357	    function onKey(e) {
358	      if (e.key !== 'Escape') return
359	      setIsOpen(false)
360	      setTitle('')
361	      setAssignee('')
362	      setValidationError(null)
363	      setApiError(null)
364	    }
365	    document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey)
366	    return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey)
367	  }, [isOpen])
368	
369	  function handleOpen() {
370	    setIsOpen(true)
371	  }
372	
373	  // handleClose resets ALL state and collapses the form.
374	  // Used by: Cancel button, successful submission.
375	  // The Escape effect duplicates this inline to avoid dep-array issues with a
376	  // non-memoized function reference.
377	  function handleClose() {
378	    setIsOpen(false)
379	    setTitle('')
380	    setAssignee('')
381	    setValidationError(null)
382	    setApiError(null)
383	  }
384	
385	  function handleTitleChange(e) {
386	    setTitle(e.target.value)
387	    // Clear both error types so the alert disappears as soon as the user edits
388	    if (validationError) setValidationError(null)
389	    if (apiError) setApiError(null)
390	  }
391	
392	  async function handleSubmit(e) {
393	    e.preventDefault()
394	    if (!title.trim()) {
395	      setValidationError('Title is required')
396	      return
397	    }
398	    setIsSubmitting(true)
399	    setApiError(null)
400	    try {
401	      await onSubmit({ title: title.trim(), assignee: assignee.trim() || null })
402	      handleClose()
403	    } catch (err) {
404	      setApiError(err.message)
405	    } finally {
406	      setIsSubmitting(false)
407	    }
408	  }
409	
410	  const displayError = validationError ?? apiError
411	
412	  if (!isOpen) {
413	    return (
414	      <div className="create-card-form">
415	        <button className="create-card-form-toggle" onClick={handleOpen}>
416	          + Add card
417	        </button>
418	      </div>
419	    )
420	  }
421	
422	  return (
423	    <div className="create-card-form create-card-form--open">
424	      <form className="create-card-form-body" onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
425	        <label htmlFor="ccf-title">Title</label>
426	        <input
427	          id="ccf-title"
428	          ref={titleInputRef}
429	          aria-label="Title"
430	          value={title}
431	          onChange={handleTitleChange}
432	          disabled={isSubmitting}
433	        />
434	
435	        <label htmlFor="ccf-assignee">Assignee</label>
436	        <input
437	          id="ccf-assignee"
438	          aria-label="Assignee"
439	          value={assignee}
440	          onChange={e => setAssignee(e.target.value)}
441	          disabled={isSubmitting}
442	        />
443	
444	        {displayError && (
445	          <p role="alert" className="create-card-form-error">{displayError}</p>
446	        )}
447	
448	        <div className="create-card-form-actions">
449	          <button
450	            type="submit"
451	            className="create-card-form-submit"
452	            disabled={isSubmitting}
453	          >
454	            {isSubmitting ? 'Adding…' : 'Add card'}
455	          </button>
456	          <button
457	            type="button"
458	            className="create-card-form-cancel"
459	            onClick={handleClose}
460	            disabled={isSubmitting}
461	          >
462	            Cancel
463	          </button>
464	        </div>
465	      </form>
466	    </div>
467	  )
468	}
469	```
470	
471	**Key notes:**
472	- `validationError` and `apiError` are mutually exclusive: validation fires before the API call; the API error is cleared at the start of each new submit attempt (`setApiError(null)`)
473	- `displayError = validationError ?? apiError` — only one is ever set; single `role="alert"` in the DOM
474	- Escape effect uses inline setters (not `handleClose`) to keep `[isOpen]` as the sole dep, matching the `CardModal.jsx` pattern
475	- `handleClose` is used by Cancel button and successful submission (same logic, co-located)
